beats.narration_text: Bonjour — this is Liam, in for Bear. Ask Claude for art, and the first thing it writes is a manifesto. Not the code. Not the artwork. A manifesto for a named generative art movement. Only then does it write the algorithm. That is not a metaphor — it is literally the instruction file. Let me show you the strangest, smartest skill Anthropic ships.

props.command: Show me the algorithmic-art skill — how does it actually work, step by step?
output: Step 1: Claude founds a named art movement. Writes the manifesto.
Step 2: Claude writes the p5.js algorithm that lives up to it.
Output: one .md manifesto + one self-contained .html artifact.

beats.narration_text: A skill is a folder of instructions. Claude reads it before it works. This particular folder is called algorithmic-art, and it rewires the request 'draw me something' into a two-step pipeline. Step one: philosophy. Step two: code. The output is a dot-md manifesto and a single self-contained HTML artifact running p5.js inline. The terracotta arrow between the two phases is the interesting part — that handoff is where the invention happens. Phase one Claude writes a brief for phase two Claude.

beats.act: Act 2 — the manifesto step / Organic Turbulence showpiece
beats.narration_text: Phase one: Claude must invent a named movement. The skill lists its own examples. 'Organic Turbulence' — chaos constrained by natural law, order emerging from disorder. Thousands of particles following vector forces generated by layered Perlin noise. Fast particles brighten toward terracotta, slow ones fade to ink shadow. This is running live right now, seeded at 42, deterministic. Not a screenshot, not a render of some stock art. This is the kind of system the skill produces. The manifesto, made real.

shot.new_visual_element: Organic Turbulence — live flow field, seeded

beats.narration_text: The skill lists four other movements. Quantum Harmonics — gridded particles with evolving phase, interference building emergent mandalas. Recursive Whispers — branching constrained by golden ratios, line weight thinning per recursion level. Field Dynamics — particles born at edges, dying at equilibrium, only the traces remain. Stochastic Crystallization — random points relaxing into Voronoi cells, random yet inevitable tiling. Each one is a written philosophy before it is a single line of code. The manifesto is a prompt that Claude writes for itself. Phase one Claude briefs phase two Claude — and that is the whole design.

Act three of the skill's own pipeline: the conceptual seed. The skill demands a hidden reference — the user's actual subject, woven invisibly into the parameters and behaviors. The skill's exact words: 'Think like a jazz musician quoting another song through algorithmic harmony — only those who know will catch it, but everyone appreciates the generative beauty.' In this flow field, the rotation constant is derived from phi, 1.618 — the golden ratio. It is in the parameters, not on the canvas. Sophistication is not a style choice; it is a design requirement.

shot.new_visual_element: Hidden seed — annotated flow field with jazz quote

beats.act: Act 4 — the code step / Art Blocks seed pattern
beats.narration_text: Phase two: p5.js. And the Art Blocks pattern — randomSeed and noiseSeed, both set to the same value. Same seed, identical output, forever. This is not a coincidence. This is a contract. The parameters must emerge from the philosophy — the skill's exact framing is 'how to express this philosophy through code?' — never chosen from a pattern menu. The philosophy writes the spec. The code implements it.

shot.new_visual_element: Onda code block — Art Blocks seed pattern

props.title: sketch.js — Art Blocks pattern

beats.narration_text: The algorithm is the plate. The seed is the print. Here is one algorithm, run with nine different seeds. Visibly the same species — visibly different individuals. Same branching patterns, same color logic, same field dynamics. Just different starting conditions. And seed 42, tile four — run it again with the same seed: pixel-identical. The skill calls this 'a series of prints from the same plate.' That is not a metaphor. That is the deliverable.

shot.new_visual_element: 3×3 seed grid — same algorithm, nine seeds

beats.act: Act 4 — fixed chrome vs variable algorithm
beats.narration_text: The artifact viewer your audience sees. Seed navigation, parameter sliders, color controls, action buttons — that sidebar is fixed. Anthropic's. The algorithm inside is free. Brand consistency outside, creative anarchy inside. And while the sidebar holds steady, watch what happens inside. The system swaps. Different movement, different parameters, completely different visual language — same chrome wrapping it. Which is the same law this video runs on: the Claude skin is fixed; the content is yours.

shot.new_visual_element: Artifact viewer mock — FIXED sidebar, VARIABLE algorithm

beats.act: Act 5 — the quality dial / Teardown moment
beats.narration_text: The Teardown moment. The skill repeats certain phrases. 'Meticulously crafted algorithm.' 'The product of deep computational expertise.' 'Painstaking optimization.' 'Master-level implementation.' It says them over and over — not as marketing, but as instructions. Why? Because repetition steers a language model's quality register. This is Anthropic prompting Claude the way power users prompt Claude: with explicit quality standards baked into the instruction file. Honest caveat: framing is not competence. The skill also bans random RGB, demands color harmony, composition, and reproducibility. The algorithm still has to earn it.

shot.new_visual_element: Quality dial — stacking phrases with terracotta counters

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The algorithmic-art skill is a two-phase pipeline: philosophy first, code second. Phase one Claude writes a manifesto for a named movement — that document is the prompt for phase two Claude. Phase two translates the philosophy into p5.js. The Art Blocks seed pattern makes every output reproducible: same seed, identical output, forever. The viewer chrome is fixed by Anthropic; the algorithm inside is free — brand consistency outside, creative anarchy inside. And the skill's quality register is steered by repetition: explicit standards repeated in the instruction file coach Claude's output toward the level the skill demands.

artifactLines: Philosophy before code: Claude writes the manifesto first; that document is the prompt for the implementation phase.
The Art Blocks seed pattern (randomSeed + noiseSeed) makes every output reproducible: same seed, identical output, forever.
The viewer chrome is fixed by Anthropic; the algorithm inside is free — brand consistency outside, creative anarchy inside.
The skill's quality dial is set by repetition: explicit standards repeated in the instruction file steer Claude's output register.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Here is the prompt — read it with me. 'Create algorithmic art about something I love. First write me the manifesto for a new generative art movement, then build it as an interactive sketch — and hide one reference in the algorithm only I would catch.' That last clause is the skill working on you: you have to think about what is worth hiding before you even type. That is not a UX feature. That is the philosophy step, happening in your own head. Run this in your own Claude session. See what movement it founds. Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: Create algorithmic art about something I love. First write me the manifesto for a new generative art movement, then build it as an interactive sketch — and hide one reference in the algorithm only I would catch.

beats.narration_text: Claude, Seeded. Liam, in for Bear.
